system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

{"code": "import subprocess, json

org = "IvanWeissVanDerPol"

# Repos list
r = subprocess.run(["gh", "repo", "list", org, "--limit", "200", "--json", "name,description,isFork,isPrivate,visibility,primaryLanguage,defaultBranch,updatedAt,pushedAt,createdAt"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
repos = json.loads(r.stdout)
print(f"TOTAL REPOS: {len(repos)}")
for rp in sorted(repos, key=lambda x: x.get("pushedAt",""), reverse=True):

    fork = "🍴" if rp["isFork"] else "  "
    print(f"  {vis}{fork} {rp['name']:40s} {lang:12s} {rp['defaultBranch']:10s} pushed:{rp['pushedAt'][:10]}")
"}

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "import subprocess, json

org = "IvanWeissVanDerPol"
r = subprocess.run(["gh", "org", "list"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
print("ORGS:", r.stdout)

# Get org settings
r2 = subprocess.run(["gh", "api", f"/orgs/{org}", "--jq", "{name,login,description,email,location,created_at,plan:{name},default_repository_permission,members_can_create_repositories,blog,twitter_username}"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
print("ORG SETTINGS:", r2.stdout)
if r2.stderr.strip():
    print("ERR:", r2.stderr[:500])

# Members
r3 = subprocess.run(["gh", "api", f"/orgs/{org}/members", "--jq", ".[].login"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15)
print("MEMBERS:", r3.stdout.strip() or "none")
"}

{"output": "=== paragu-ai-builder workflow contents ===
--- ci.yml ---
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  quality:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps

      - name: TypeScript check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit

      - name: Lint
        run: npx next lint --max-warnings 50

      - name: Check generated renderer map is up to date
        run: npm run generate:renderer && git diff --exit-code web/lib/engine/generated/renderer-map.ts

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://paragu-ai.com' }}
===
--- deploy.yml ---
name: Deploy

# Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys from the GitHub repo — no CI token needed.
# This workflow only handles the VPS (Docker Swarm) deployment.
on:
  push:
    branches: [Main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: deploy-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  vps:
    name: VPS Hostinger
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup SSH
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set +e
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "${{ secrets.VPS_SSH_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
          ssh-keyscan -H 72.61.44.159 >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null || true

      - name: Run deploy script on VPS
        run: |
          ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new root@72.61.44.159 'bash /opt/stacks/paragu-ai-builder/deploy.sh prod'
===
--- pr-checks.yml ---
name: PR Checks

# Single merge gate for all PRs. Replaces the old set of overlapping
# workflows (ci.yml, ci-cd.yml, test.yml, validate.yml, quality-gates.yml)
# which all ran lint + typecheck + tests separately and wasted compute.
#
# Required on Main and staging via branch protection rules.

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [Main, staging]
  push:
    branches: [Main, staging]

concurrency:
  # Cancel superseded runs on the same PR — saves compute when multiple
  # pushes land quickly. Main/staging pushes don't cancel each other.
  group: pr-checks-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # 1. Lint + Typecheck — cheapest, runs first, blocks other jobs
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  lint-typecheck:
    name: Lint & Typecheck
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint
      - name: Typecheck
        run: npm run typecheck

   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   #2. Schema validation — ensures every registry entry has a schema
   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   validate-schemas:
     name: Validate Schemas
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 5
     needs: lint-typecheck
     defaults:
       run:
         working-directory: ./
     steps:
       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
       - name: Validate registry has schemas
         run: npm run validate:schemas

   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   #3. Tenant-config validation — catches bad page JSON / content refs /
   #    missing locale content before a runtime error bites a tenant.
   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   validate:
     name: Validate Tenant Configs
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 10
     needs: [lint-typecheck, validate-schemas]
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Validate tenant sites
        # TEMPORARY: allow machine-translated locales until nexa-paraguay's
        # German is professionally translated (tracked in sites/nexa-paraguay/
        # docs/LAUNCH.md item #8).
        env:
          ALLOW_MACHINE_TRANSLATIONS: '1'
        run: npm run validate:sites
      - name: Validate content placeholders
        run: npm run validate:content
      - name: Validate relocacion vertical
        run: npm run validate:vertical relocacion

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # 3. Unit + integration tests with coverage upload
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    needs: lint-typecheck
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    env:
      # Placeholders so code that reads env at module-load doesn't crash.
      # No real Supabase calls happen in unit tests.
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: [REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: placeholder-anon-key
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Run tests
        run: npm run test:unit
      - name: Upload coverage
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage
          path: web/coverage/
          retention-days: 7

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # 4. Build smoke — verifies `npm run build` succeeds end-to-end.
  #    Runs after lint+typecheck to avoid wasting a slow build when
  #    the cheap checks already failed.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    needs: lint-typecheck
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    env:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: [REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: placeholder-anon-key
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # 5. Secrets scan — blocks PRs that accidentally commit API keys /
  #    tokens. TruffleHog flags high-entropy strings above threshold.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  secrets-scan:
    name: Secrets Scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # Full history so TruffleHog can diff base..HEAD.
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: TruffleHog Secrets Scan
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          base: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
          head: HEAD
          # `--only-verified` reports only secrets TruffleHog could
          # confirm against their originating service. Quieter than the
          # entropy heuristic and doesn't have the CLI-arg bug that was
          # failing every PR (--filter-entropy requires a value).
          extra_args: --only-verified
===
--- nightly.yml ---
name: Nightly

# Off-path checks that shouldn't block PRs but are worth knowing about.
# Runs once a day at ~03:00 UTC (00:00 Asunción). Also on manual dispatch.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 3 * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Dependency audit — flags known CVEs in our deps. Doesn't fail the
  # build (audit has a lot of noise at `moderate`), just logs + creates
  # an issue when `high` or `critical` show up so a human can triage.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  audit:
    name: npm audit
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Audit (high+)
        # Fails only on high/critical — moderate CVEs are logged and
        # checked manually in the review.
        run: npm audit --audit-level=high

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Full E2E (moved off the PR path — Playwright takes ~8 min and has
  # historical flakiness so it lived as `continue-on-error` before).
  # Runs nightly against the built app. If we start trusting it, promote
  # back to pr-checks.yml.
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  e2e:
    name: Playwright E2E
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    env:
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: [REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: placeholder-anon-key
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Run E2E
        run: npm run test:e2e
        continue-on-error: true
      - name: Upload report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: e2e-report
          path: web/playwright-report
          retention-days: 14
===
--- lighthouse.yml ---
name: Lighthouse CI

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'web/components/**'
      - 'web/lib/engine/**'
      - 'web/lib/tokens/**'
      - 'web/app/**'
      - 'sites/**'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  lighthouse:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci

      - name: Install Lighthouse CI
        run: npm i -g @lhci/cli@0.14.x

      - name: Build + run Lighthouse against 3 representative pages
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: http://localhost:3000
        run: lhci autorun --config=./lighthouserc.json

      - name: Upload Lighthouse report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: lighthouse-report
          path: web/.lighthouseci/
          retention-days: 7
===
--- cache-purge.yml ---
name: Cache purge (post-merge)

# Purges Cloudflare edge cache after a Main merge lands. Without this,
# the public site serves stale content for up to 24 hours via our
# stale-while-revalidate config (s-maxage=60, SWR=86400). Relies on:
#
#   Secrets:
#     CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN  — scoped to "Zone:Cache Purge"
#     CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID    — paragu-ai.com zone id
#
# If either secret is missing the job is a no-op (exit 0) so this
# workflow never blocks a merge.

on:
  push:
    branches: [Main]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  purge:
    name: Purge Cloudflare cache
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 3
    steps:
      - name: Purge everything
        env:
          CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" ] || [ -z "$CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID" ]; then
            echo "::notice::Cache-purge secrets not configured — skipping purge."
            exit 0
          fi

          response=$(curl -sS -X POST \
            "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/$CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID/purge_cache" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUD...KEN" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            --data '{"purge_everything":true}')

          echo "$response"
          success=$(echo "$response" | grep -o '"success":[^,}]*' | head -1 | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d ' ')
          if [ "$success" != "true" ]; then
            echo "::error::Cache purge failed"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::notice::Cache purged successfully"
===
--- preview-deploy.yml ---
name: Preview Deploy

# Deploys every PR branch to a unique Cloudflare Pages preview URL
# so clients and team can review changes before merging.

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

concurrency:
  group: preview-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  preview:
    name: Deploy preview
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: web
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: web/package-lock.json

      - name: Install
        run: npm ci --legacy-peer-deps

      - name: TypeScript check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL: ${{ vars.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://paragu-ai.com' }}

      - name: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages preview
        uses: cloudflare/wrangler-action@v3
        with:
          apiToken: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
          accountId: ${{ vars.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
          command: pages deploy .open-next --project-name=paragu-ai-builder --branch=${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}

      - name: Comment PR with preview URL
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const pr = context.payload.pull_request;
            if (!pr) return;
            const branch = pr.head.ref;
            const previewUrl = `https://${branch}.paragu-ai-builder.pages.dev`;
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: pr.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: `🚀 Preview deployed: [${previewUrl}](${previewUrl})`,
            });

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:

--- ci.yml ---
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  lint-typecheck-build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm lint
      - run: pnpm tsc --noEmit
      - run: pnpm build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: [REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: placeholder
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm test -- --run

          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: [REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]

===
--- deploy.yml ---
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /tmp && rm -rf fun4me && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/fun4me.git
            cd fun4me && docker build \
              --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }} \
              --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }} \
              -t fun4me:prod .
            docker service update --force fun4me_web
===
=== aiw-infra CI ===
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check YAML syntax
        run: |
          for f in stacks/**/docker-stack.yml stacks/**/docker-compose.yml configs/**/*.yml; do
            python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open('$f'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "INVALID: $f"
          done

      - name: Check shell scripts
        run: |
          for f in scripts/*.sh deploy.sh; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && bash -n "$f" || echo "SYNTAX ERROR: $f"
          done

      - name: Check Python syntax
        run: |
          for f in scripts/*.py services/**/app.py; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && python3 -m py_compile "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo "SYNTAX ERROR: $f"
          done

      - name: Detect secrets
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          path: ./
          base: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
          head: HEAD

  docker-compose-validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install Docker Compose
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.docker/cli-plugins
          curl -SL https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/v2.24.0/docker-compose-linux-x86_64 -o ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose
          chmod +x ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose

      - name: Validate Docker configs
        run: |
          for f in stacks/**/docker-stack.yml stacks/**/docker-compose.yml; do
            docker compose -f "$f" config --quiet && echo "OK: $f" || echo "INVALID: $f"
          done

  gitignore-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Check .env is gitignored
        run: |
          grep -q '^\.env$' .gitignore && echo ".env is gitignored" || (echo ".env NOT gitignored!"; exit 1)
          grep -q '^\.env.template$' .gitignore && echo ".env.template is gitignored" || echo "NOTE: .env.template is intentionally not gitignored"", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

## CI/CD ARCHITECTURE

### Workflow Totals by Repo
| Repo | Workflows | Deployment |
|------|-----------|------------|
| paragu-ai-builder | **8** | CF Pages + VPS (Docker) |
| fun4me | 2 | VPS Docker Swarm |
| elviajero-comercio | 1 | VPS Docker Swarm |
| aiw-infra | 2 | CI only (no deploy) |

### Patterns:
1. **paragu-ai-builder** (gold standard) — CI (lint+typecheck+build), PR Checks (5 jobs: lint/typecheck, schema validation, tenant validation, tests, secrets scan, build, e2e), Preview Deploy (CF Pages per branch), Nightly (audit+E2E), Lighthouse CI, Cache Purge (post-merge CF)
2. **fun4me** — Simple CI (lint+typecheck+build, tests) + deploy via SSH to Docker
3. **elviajero-comercio** — Single deploy workflow via SSH
4. **aiw-infra** — YAML/shell/Python syntax validation + secrets scan
5. **nexa-paraguay, golden-visa-advisory, superspuma, etc.** — **No CI/CD at all**

### Infrastructure: 1 VPS (72.61.44.159), Docker Swarm, Cloudflare Pages
- Deploy secrets: VPS_SSH_KEY, DEPLOY_KEY, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN

---

## KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHTS

### paragu-ai-builder (8.4M lines TypeScript)
- AI-powered multi-tenant website builder for PY businesses
- 8 CI/CD workflows: quality gates, tenant validation, preview deploy, nightly E2E, Lighthouse
- VPS + Cloudflare Pages dual deployment
- Supabase backend

### paragu-ai (monorepo)
- @ai-whisperers/theme, /content, /ui, /seo, /whatsapp shared packages
- Powers multi-tenant site generation

### elviajero-comercio
- Flagship e-commerce: 104+ components, admin panel, auth, 4 payment methods, i18n, PWA
- Reference standard for @aiw/* package extraction

### ma-research-pipeline
- Most active: pushed [REDACTED_PHONE]. Autonomous M&A research with 25+ data sources
- 8-dimension scoring, self-improving agents

### Security Gaps
- **Org secrets: 0** — everything on repo-level only
- **No branch protection** on any repo
- **No webhooks** configured
- **No environments** configured
- Default workflow permissions = write (can approve PRs)

### Publishing Approach
- Fun4me, clínica-duerksen, alejandro-villamayor: standalone public mirrors
- Client sites (superspuma, golden-visa, etc.): private, no CI/CD
- paragu-ai-builder: private but has the most sophisticated pipeline

---

## MISSING/UNUSED FEATURES
- [ ] Repository topics / tags on all repos
- [ ] GitHub Environments (0 configured)
- [ ] Branch protection rules
- [ ] Org-level secrets (0)
- [ ] Webhooks / integrations
- [ ] Dependabot (0 secrets, only on builder+infra)
- [ ] Code owners
- [ ] Issue templates on most repos
- [ ] Release / tag management
- [ ] GitHub Pages deployments (only personal site)
- [ ] GitHub Projects / Discussions
- [ ] Package registry (npm/GitHub Packages)
"""

print(report)

=== priorities README ===
# ParaguAI — Operations Base

**Single source of truth.** Repos, clients, sites, infra, cron, contacts, issues.

## Index

| File | What's Inside |
|------|---------------|
| [client-repos.md](client-repos.md) | Every repo: client, framework, last commit, GH link, status, deep details |
| [clients.md](clients.md) | All clients with URLs, contacts, tiers |
| [cron.md](cron.md) | All 22 cron jobs with schedules and status |
| [infrastructure.md](infrastructure.md) | Docker Swarm, Traefik, network map |
| [issues.md](issues.md) | Priority-ordered 🔴🟡🟢 issues |
| [repos.md](repos.md) | Full repo index by category |

## Quick Stats

- **19 live websites** on *.paragu-ai.com + 2 on *.sunstein.cloud
- **22 Docker Swarm services** running on 1 VPS
- **30+ Git repos** (16 client sites, 9 platform, 5 Hermes, rest research/auto)
- **22 cron jobs** — all currently in ERROR state
- **37 GitHub repos** in Ai-Whisperers org (some archived)

## Live Website Map

```
paragu-ai.com          → paragu-ai-builder (3x)
dayah.paragu-ai.com    → Dayah LitWorks
viajero.paragu-ai.com  → El Viajero Comercio

nudo.paragu-ai.com     → Nudo (metal band)
ozmontania.paragu-ai.com → Oz Montania (artist portfolio)
fun4me.paragu-ai.com   → Fun4Me (auth fixed)
superspuma.paragu-ai.com → Superspuma (mattresses)
goldenvisa.paragu-ai.com → Golden Visa Advisory
cabral.paragu-ai.com   → Granja Cabral (egg farm)
duerksen.paragu-ai.com → Clinica Duerksen (dental)
mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com → Mantra Spa
magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com → Magnolia Peluqueria
maiyu.paragu-ai.com    → Maiyu Atelier
villamayor.paragu-ai.com → Villamayor & Asociados (law)
30vcs.paragu-ai.com    → 30vcs (placeholder)
brahm.paragu-ai.com    → Brahm the Racoon
nicolas-duarte.paragu-ai.com → Nicolas Duarte (portfolio)
space.sunstein.cloud   → Space Agent (AI agent)
gyro.sunstein.cloud    → Gyro (container)
```

# Ai-Whisperers — Complete Org Overhaul Plan

**Date:** [REDACTED_PHONE]
**Repos total:** 54 → **After cleanup:** ~36 active + 1 base repo
**Goal:** Un solo repo base de packages npm, clientes independientes, upgrades propagados.

---

## 1. Current State

### 54 repos en la org

| Categoría | Cantidad | Repos |
|-----------|----------|-------|
| **Client sites activos** | ~24 | elviajero, nexa, golden-visa, superspuma, dayah, fun4me, 3md, nudo, granja-cabral, villamayor, ozmontania, depiflash, stroopwafel-huis, maiyu-atelier, de-abasto-a-casa, alejandro-villamayor, polki-squad, bufete-mendez, stoicfinch, nico-portfolio, clinica-duerksen, Vete |
| **Auto-generated leads** | 5 | magnolia-peluqueria, luis-de-leon-concept, mantra-spa, cocodrilo-fitness, bichos-gym |
| **Templates** | 3 | template-nextjs-client, client-kit, repo-template |
| **Core platform** | 2 | paragu-ai-builder, paragu-ai |
| **Research/Data** | 5 | ma-research-pipeline, paragu-ai-leads, solstein-v2, telescope-ai, aiw-docs |
| **Infra** | 4 | priorities, aiw-infra, code-agent, code-agent-ui |
| **Archived/stale** | ~11 | paragu-ai-platform, fun4me-store, solstein-v1-archive, lealtis, refugio-animal, photos-to-kml, solstein-mvp-demo, infra-cost-tracker, portfolio-website, energy21, [REMOVED]-unhackathon |

### Problemas identificados

1. **Sin base compartida** — cada cliente duplica código. elviajero tiene 104 componentes, golden-visa tiene 3.
2. **elviajero es el repo más avanzado** — tiene admin (18 páginas), auth SSO, 4 payment gateways, i18n, shadcn/ui, Docker healthcheck, PWA, SEO completo. Los demás no tienen nada de esto.
3. **paragu-ai monorepo no funciona** — no tiene workspaces, solo typescript como dependencia.
4. **Cero CI/CD** — ningún repo tiene GitHub Actions.
5. **Template atrasado** — template-nextjs-client no tiene shadcn/ui, supabase, ni auth.
6. **client-kit infrautilizado** — existe pero casi ningún cliente lo usa.

---

## 2. Target Architecture

```

│

│

├── nexa-paraguay                 ← installa @ai-whisperers/*

   - superspuma, dayah, etc: even less
   - Every client repo reinvents the same patterns
   - client-kit exists but barely used (5 components, only superspuma+3md bundle it)

B) paragu-ai monorepo is BROKEN
   - Has apps/ structure but NO npm workspaces
   - Only TypeScript as shared dep
   - clinica-duerksen lives inside it as a sub-app but isn't independently deployable
   - Effectively a copy-paste dumping ground

C) paragu-ai-builder is the REAL platform (8.4M lines!)
   - AI-powered multi-tenant builder
   - Generates sites under /sites/ directory
   - Has full CI/CD pipeline (8 workflows)
   - But clients under /sites/ don't have their own CI/CD
   - Sites are generated JSON configs + built output, NOT reusable packages

D) Client sites have NO CI/CD (17 of 19)
   - Only elviajero and fun4me deploy via GH Actions
   - All others: manual deploy or cron-based
   - Zero tests on any client site

E) Security is nonexistent

=== 3. INFRASTRUCTURE ===

messages.content: {"todos": [{"id": "1", "content": "Deep-dive analysis complete", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "2", "content": "Identify bottlenecks, redundancies, risks", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "3", "content": "Propose actionable update plan with options", "status": "in_progress"}], "summary": {"total": 3, "pending": 0, "in_progress": 1, "completed": 2, "cancelled": 0}}

Here's the definitive analysis and decision framework.

---

## Complete Diagnosis

### The 5 Real Problems

**1. You have TWO platforms fighting each other**
- `paragu-ai-builder` (8.4M TS, 478MB) — AI multi-tenant builder, generates sites under `/sites/`, has full CI/CD
- `paragu-ai` monorepo — supposed to be the shared platform, but has NO npm workspaces, is effectively dead code
- Neither talks to the other. Builder regenerates sites, monorepo just sits there.

**2. 17/19 client sites have zero CI/CD**
- Only elviajero-comercio and fun4me deploy via GH Actions
- All others: manual or cron. Deploying a client site is a copy-paste operation

**3. Massive code duplication without a release mechanism**
- elviajero has 104 components. golden-visa has 3. superspuma has 8.
- `client-kit` exists (5 components) but isn't published to npm — every repo has to copy it locally
- Updating a shared component means touching every repo manually

**4. paragu-ai monorepo is a hollow shell**
- Has `apps/clinica-duerksen` as a subfolder but no workspaces config
- No turbo.json, no shared build, no packages directory
- It's a folder structure pretending to be a monorepo

**5. No security layer at all**
- 0 org secrets, 0 branch protections, 0 environments, 0 webhooks
- SSH keys embedded in workflow YAML
- Any compromised token = full infrastructure access

### The Leverage Point

Your builder works. The constraint isn't tech — it's that every site is disconnected. The fix isn't to rebuild anything. It's to **extract the shared code into a publishable package** and **add CI/CD to every client site**.

---

## Two Paths

### Path A: The Org Overhaul (already planned)
The plan at `/root/aiw-org-overhaul-plan.md` — create `@aiw/base` monorepo with 8 npm packages (ui, commerce, admin, auth, whatsapp, seo, i18n, theme), extract from elviajero, install in all clients.

- **Cost:** 10-12 days, 8 packages, archive 18 repos
- **Benefit:** Centralized upgrades, consistent UX, reduced code
- **Risk:** Big upfront investment, builder-generated sites (under `/sites/`) won't benefit until rebuilt

### Path B: Ship the Builder Outputs + Minimal Wrapper
Leave the builder as-is. Make each generated site independently deployable with a thin wrapper:
1. Each site gets a `Dockerfile` + `deploy.yml` (copy from fun4me's pattern ~20 lines)
2. Add a shared `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` published to npm (5-10 components, not 8 packages)
3. CI/CD to every client repo in 1 day

- **Cost:** 1-2 days to set up CI/CD across all clients
- **Benefit:** Every site deployable, shared components published, minimal disruption
- **Risk:** Won't solve the builder vs monorepo split, slow for non-builder clients (elviajero)

---

**Which path do you want?** A) Full org overhaul (10-12 days, 8 packages, archive 18 repos) or B) Ship the builder + CI/CD to every client (1-2 days, one package, minimal change)

Or a hybrid: do B this week, then A when the builder clients stabilize.

{"output": "=== fun4me deploy.yml full ===
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /tmp && rm -rf fun4me && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/fun4me.git
            cd fun4me && docker build \
              --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${{ secrets.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL }} \

=== aiw-infra ci.yml ===
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Check YAML syntax
        run: |
          for f in stacks/**/docker-stack.yml stacks/**/docker-compose.yml configs/**/*.yml; do
            python3 -c "import yaml, sys; yaml.safe_load(open('$f'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "INVALID: $f"
          done

      - name: Check shell scripts
        run: |
          for f in scripts/*.sh deploy.sh; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && bash -n "$f" || echo "SYNTAX ERROR: $f"
          done

      - name: Check Python syntax
        run: |
          for f in scripts/*.py services/**/app.py; do
            [ -f "$f" ] && python3 -m py_compile "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo "SYNTAX ERROR: $f"
          done

      - name: Detect secrets
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          path: ./
          base: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
          head: HEAD

  docker-compose-validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install Docker Compose
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.docker/cli-plugins
          curl -SL https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/download/v2.24.0/docker-compose-linux-x86_64 -o ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose
          chmod +x ~/.docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose

      - name: Validate Docker configs
        run: |
          for f in stacks/**/docker-stack.yml stacks/**/docker-compose.yml; do
            docker compose -f "$f" config --quiet && echo "OK: $f" || echo "INVALID: $f"
          done

  gitignore-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Check .env is gitignored
        run: |
          grep -q '^\.env$' .gitignore && echo ".env is gitignored" || (echo ".env NOT gitignored!"; exit 1)
          grep -q '^\.env.template$' .gitignore && echo ".env.template is gitignored" || echo "NOTE: .env.template is intentionally not gitignored"", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

      - name: Detect secrets
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main

          head: HEAD

{"path": "/root/aiw-github-update-plan.md", "content": "# AI Whisperers — Complete GitHub Update Plan

## Context

paragu-ai monorepo removed (was dead — no workspaces, not used).
67 repos total. 19 active client sites. Most have zero CI/CD.
Now: build a **reusable CI/CD repo** imported by all clients, harden security, clean up the org.

---

## Phase 1: Central CI/CD Repo (`ci-cd`)

**New repo:** `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd`
**Purpose:** Single source of truth for all workflows. Update once, propagate everywhere.

### Structure

```
ci-cd/
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── deploy-client-site.yml      ← reusable workflow (called by clients)
│   ├── deploy-paragu-ai-builder.yml
│   ├── ci-nextjs.yml               ← reusable: lint + typecheck + build + test
│   ├── ci-python.yml               ← reusable: ruff + pytest
│   ├── security-scan.yml           ← reusable: trufflehog + npm audit
│   └── sync-workflows.yml          ← pushes latest workflows to all client repos
├── scripts/
│   ├── deploy-vps.sh               ← the actual SSH deploy script (kept here, not in YAML)
│   └── sync-workflows.sh           ← copies .github/workflows/* to all client repos
├── templates/
│   ├── Dockerfile.nextjs           ← standard Next.js multi-stage Dockerfile
│   ├── docker-compose.yml          ← standard client Docker Compose
│   ├── .env.template
│   └── README.md
└── clients.json                    ← map of repo -> deploy config
```

### How It Works

1. **Reusable workflows** — each client repo adds ONE file `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:
   ```yaml
   name: Deploy
   on:
     push:
       branches: [main]
   jobs:
     deploy:
       uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-client-site.yml@main
       with:
         site-name: fun4me
         site-url: https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com
       secrets: inherit
   ```

2. **`sync-workflows` workflow** — pushes latest workflow versions to all client repos on push to ci-cd main. No manual copy-paste.

3. **`clients.json`** — central registry:
   ```json
   {
     "fun4me": {
       "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/fun4me",
       "url": "https://fun4me.paragu-ai.com",
       "package-manager": "pnpm",
       "node-version": "22",
       "docker-image": "fun4me:prod",
       "docker-service": "fun4me_web",
       "build-args": ["NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL", "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY", "NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL"]
     },
     "elviajero-comercio": {
       "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio",
       "url": "https://viajero.paragu-ai.com",
       "package-manager": "npm",
       "node-version": "20",
       "docker-image": "elviajero-comercio:prod",
       "docker-service": "elviajero-comercio_web",
       "deploy-strategy": "git-pull"  // different from build-new below
     },
     ...
   }
   ```

### Workflow Matrix

| Workflow | Trigger | What It Does |
|----------|---------|--------------|
| `ci-nextjs.yml` | PR + push to main | npm/pnpm install → lint → typecheck → build → test → trufflehog |
| `ci-python.yml` | PR + push to main | ruff check → pytest → trufflehog |
| `deploy-client-site.yml` | push to main (called) | build Docker image on VPS → `docker service update --force` |
| `deploy-paragu-ai-builder.yml` | push to main | CF Pages deploy + VPS deploy |
| `security-scan.yml` | PR + scheduled weekly | trufflehog + npm audit + docker scan |
| `sync-workflows.yml` | push to ci-cd main | copies workflows to all client repos via GH API |

---

## Phase 2: Security Hardening

### Must Fix (week 1)
- [ ] **Set org-level secrets** — VPS_HOST, VPS_USER, VPS_KEY, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- [ ] **Update ci-cd workflows** to use org secrets via `secrets: inherit` — no more inline secrets in YAML files
- [ ] **Remove inline SSH keys** from elviajero and fun4me deploy.yml
- [ ] **Add branch protection** to elviajero, fun4me, paragu-ai-builder, paragu-ai-leads, ma-research-pipeline
  - Require PR for main, require status checks, require signed commits

### Should Fix (week 2)
- [ ] **GitHub Environments** — create `production` env with VPS_HOST/VPS_KEY required only from that env
- [ ] **Change default workflow permissions** to `read` from `write`
- [ ] **Add CODEOWNERS** to every active repo
- [ ] **Set up Dependabot** on all active repos (not just builder + infra)
- [ ] **Add security.md vulnerability reporting**
- [ ] **Audit repo access** — verify only needed members per repo

### Nice to Have
- [ ] **Webhook to Hermes** on security alert / Dependabot alert
- [ ] **Scheduled security audit** (1x/week via cron)
- [ ] **Signed commits enforcement**

---

## Phase 3: Org Cleanup

### Archive List (no longer needed)
- `stroopwafel-huis` — business plan, not code. Move to docs.
- `nicolas-duarte-career` — merged into nicolas-duarte-site
- `nico-duarte`, `nico` — empty, superseded
- `pizzeria-ejemplo` — example, not needed
- `grocery` — abandoned experiment
- `contract-analyzer` — not actively used
- `Johns-Grimoire` — personal notes
- `wizard-academy` — abandoned
- `Marketing-Agent` — abandoned
- `Company-Repos-Understanding` — one-off research
- All archived repos currently in org: confirm they stay archived

### Topics to Add (every active repo needs)
- `client-site`, `platform`, `tool`, `research`, `infrastructure`, `template`
- Language tags: `typescript`, `python`, `java`
- Business tags: `ecommerce`, `healthcare`, `legal`, `beauty`, `music`, `agency`

### Repo Naming Cleanup
- `3md-website` → `3md-creative-agency` (descriptive)
- `BDSM-Paraguay-Toolkit` → `bdsm-paraguay-toolkit` (lowercase, no caps)
- `Ivan_Weiss_Portfolio` → `ivan-weiss-portfolio` (consistent kebab-case)
- Note: renaming repos breaks URLs. Only do if worth it.

---

## Phase 4: Client CI/CD Rollout

One deploy.yml per client. Each client gets:

| Client | Repo | Node | PM | Deploy Strategy | Existing CI? |
|--------|------|------|----|----------------|-------------|
| El Viajero | elviajero-comercio | 20 | npm | git-pull + build on VPS | YES (deploy.yml) |
| Fun4Me | fun4me | 22 | pnpm | build locally → scp Docker image | YES (ci+deploy) |
| Nexa Paraguay | nexa-paraguay | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Golden Visa | golden-visa-advisory | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Superspuma | superspuma | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Dayah LitWorks | dayah-litworks | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Nudo | nudo | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Oz Montania | ozmontania-website | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Granja Cabral | granja-cabral | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Maiyu Atelier | maiyu-atelier | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Villamayor | villamayor-asociados | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Depiflash | depiflash | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| 3MD Agency | 3md-website | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| De Abasto a Casa | de-abasto-a-casa | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Polki Squad | polki-squad | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Bufete Mendez | bufete-mendez | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Stoicfinch | stoicfinch | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Nicolas Duarte | nico-portfolio | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |
| Clinica Duerksen | clinica-duerksen | 20 | npm | build on VPS | NO |

**Estimate:** 1 hour to create ci-cd repo with reusable workflows. 15 min per client = ~5 hours total.

---

## Phase 5: Additional Workstreams

### 5a: client-kit → Published npm Package
- Current: 5 components, local-only, copied into repos
- Target: `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` published to npm
- Minimal scope: WhatsAppFloat, SEO component, analytics, theme tokens, error boundary
- Add CI/CD to client-kit repo: `npm publish` on tag push
- Install in all 19 client repos in one batch command

### 5b: template-nextjs-client Refresh
- Currently: Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4, no shadcn/ui, no Supabase, no auth
- Needs: shadcn/ui components, Supabase SSR auth, WhatsAppFloat, SEO component, Dockerfile, deploy.yml template
- Make it THE starting point for all new client sites

### 5c: Priorities Repo Format
- Currently: markdown files. Working. Keep as-is — it's the SSOT.
- Add: `clients.json` in machine-readable format (parallel to markdown for tool consumption)
- Add: healthcheck that verifies all clients.json entries have corresponding infra

### 5d: Monitoring (aiw-infra)
- Prometheus + Grafana stack exists in `stacks/monitoring/` but not confirmed running
- Task: verify + fix if broken. Add UptimeRobot or basic cron healthchecks.
- Add disk space alert to incident-commander

### 5e: Fix the 22 Broken Cron Jobs
- All Solstein + IC + lead-scout jobs in ERROR
- Need investigation: OpenRouter credits? Model deprecation? Auth token expired?
- After fix: add self-healing (cron that checks cron health)

---

## Phase 6: Org-Level Automation

### Webhooks
- Add webhook to Hermes for: push events on main branches (deploy notifications)
- Future: webhook for PR opened on client repos (auto-preview deploy)

### GitHub Projects / Issues
- Create a "Client Sites" project board
- Each client repo gets standard issue templates (from repo-template)
- Labels: `client`, `platform`, `security`, `ci-cd`, `content`

### Release Strategy
- Tag releases on ci-cd repo (v1.0 → clients pin to @v1 or @main)
- Tag releases on client-kit (npm versioning)

---

## Timeline

| Phase | Duration | Output |
|-------|----------|--------|
| P1: ci-cd repo | 1 day | Reusable workflows, VPS deploy script, clients.json |
| P2: Security | 0.5 day | Org secrets, branch protection, remove inline keys |
| P3: Cleanup | 0.5 day | Archive stale repos, add topics |
| P4: Client rollout | 1 day | deploy.yml in all 19 client repos |
| P5a: client-kit publish | 0.5 day | npm package published |
| P5b: template refresh | 1 day | Updated template with shadcn, auth, Dockerfile |
| P5d: Monitoring | 0.5 day | Prometheus/Grafana fix, disk alert |
| P5e: Cron fix | 0.5 day | Investigate + fix all 22 jobs |
| P6: Automation | 0.5 day | Webhooks, project board, issue templates |

**Total: ~6 days** for all phases. P1-P4 (core CI/CD) in first 3 days.

---

## Next Action

Want me to build `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` first?
- Create the repo
- Write the reusable deploy workflow (with input params for site name, URL, build strategy)
- Write the sync-workflows script
- Create the standard Dockerfile template
- Create `clients.json` with all 19 client entries

Now let me analyze all CI/CD workflows across every repo that has them.

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "# Check every repo for workflows - all repos that might have CI/CD
for repo in ma-research-pipeline base elviajero-comercio nexa-paraguay de-abasto-a-casa nudo clinica-duerksen fun4me stroopwafel-huis golden-visa-advisory granja-cabral maiyu-atelier villamayor-asociados ozmontania-website 3md-website magnolia-peluqueria luis-de-leon-concept mantra-spa cocodrilo-fitness bichos-gym depiflash superspuma dayah-litworks client-kit paragu-ai-builder alejandro-villamayor polki-squad bufete-mendez priorities stoicfinch nexa-propiedades nico-portfolio paragu-ai-leads template-nextjs-client paragu-ai-platform Vete solstein-v2 code-agent code-agent-ui company .github courses-website work-coordination; do
  count=$(gh api "repos/Ai-Whisperers/$repo/actions/workflows" --jq '.total_count' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$count" != "0" ] && [ -n "$count" ]; then
    echo "=== $repo ($count workflows) ==="
    gh api "repos/Ai-Whisperers/$repo/actions/workflows" --jq '.workflows[] | "  \(.name) [\(.state)] \(.path | split("/")[-1])"' 2>/dev/null
  fi
done", "timeout": 120}

{"command": "# Check the new workflows discovered - maiyu, villamayor, dayah, ozmontania, 3md all have deploy.yml!
# Let me see what they look like
for repo in maiyu-atelier villamayor-asociados dayah-litworks ozmontania-website 3md-website template-nextjs-client base; do
  echo "=== $repo deploy.yml ==="
  gh api "repos/Ai-Whisperers/$repo/contents/.github/workflows/deploy.yml" --jq '.content' 2>&1 | base64 -d 2>/dev/null
  echo "---"
done", "timeout": 30}

messages.content:
{"output": "=== maiyu-atelier deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /tmp && rm -rf maiyu && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/maiyu-atelier.git maiyu
            cd maiyu && docker build -t maiyu:prod .
            docker service update --force maiyu_web
---
=== villamayor-asociados deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.VPS_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /tmp && rm -rf villamayor && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/villamayor-asociados.git villamayor
            cd villamayor && docker build -t villamayor:prod .
            docker service update --force villamayor_web
---
=== dayah-litworks deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.VPS_HOST }}

          key: ${{ secrets.VPS_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /tmp && rm -rf dayah && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/dayah-litworks.git dayah
            cd dayah && docker build -t dayah:prod .
            docker service update --force dayah_web
---
=== ozmontania-website deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy Oz Montanía to VPS

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3
        with:
          host: 72.61.44.159
          username: root
          key: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
          script: |
            cd /root/ozmontania-website
            git pull
            npm ci
            npm run build
            docker build -t ozmontania:prod .
            docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml ozmontania
            docker service scale ozmontania_web=0
            sleep 5
            docker service scale ozmontania_web=2
---
=== 3md-website deploy.yml ===
name: Deploy

        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1

            cd /tmp && rm -rf client && git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/superspuma.git client

---

{"output": "=== clinica-duerksen ci.yml ===
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  quality:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Type check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: "http://placeholder"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: "placeholder"
=== base ci.yml ===
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build -- --cache-dir=.turbo
      - run: npm run test
=== 3md-website correct (it clones superspuma!) ===
BUG: 3md-website deploy.yml clones superspuma instead of 3md-website
=== template-nextjs-client also wrong (clones superspuma too) ===
=== solstein-v2 CI ===
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  PYTHON_VERSION: "3.11"

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Format
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - run: uv python install ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}
      - run: uv sync --extra dev
      - run: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
      - run: uv run ruff format --check src/ tests/

  typecheck:
    name: Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

Now I have the complete picture. Here's the full analysis.

---

## Complete Org Analysis

### Repo Count

| Scope | Active | Archived | Total |
|-------|--------|----------|-------|
| Ai-Whisperers org | 63 | ~35 | 98 |
| IvanWeissVanDerPol personal | ~60 | ~7 | ~67 |
| LosingLoonies-Youtube | 3 | 0 | 3 |
| **Total** | **~126** | **~42** | **~168** |

### All CI/CD Workflows Discovered

| Repo | Workflows | Type | Notes |
|------|-----------|------|-------|
| **paragu-ai-builder** | **8** | Next.js | Gold standard. CI, PR Checks (6 jobs), Preview Deploy, Nightly, Lighthouse, Cache Purge |
| **Vete** | **10** (5 active) | Next.js | Most complex. Deploy to GCP/Docker Swarm/Vercel, CodeQL, Dependabot |
| **fun4me** | 2 | Next.js | CI (lint+typecheck+test) + Deploy (Docker Swarm) |
| **solstein-v2** | 2 | Python | CI (ruff+uv+pytest) + Dependabot |
| **base** | 1 | TS monorepo | CI (build+test) |
| **elviajero-comercio** | 1 | Next.js | Deploy only (no CI) |
| **clinica-duerksen** | 1 | Next.js | CI only (lint+typecheck+build, no deploy) |
| **maiyu-atelier** | 1 | Next.js | Deploy only |
| **villamayor-asociados** | 1 | Next.js | Deploy only |
| **ozmontania-website** | 1 | Next.js | Deploy only |
| **3md-website** | 1 | Next.js | **BUG: clones superspuma instead of 3md!** |
| **dayah-litworks** | 1 | Next.js | Deploy only |
| **template-nextjs-client** | 1 | Next.js | **Same bug: clones superspuma** |
| **paragu-ai-platform** | 2 | Archived | Published `@ai-whisperers/*` to GH Packages |
| **aiw-infra** | 2 | IaC | CI (YAML/shell validation) + Dependabot |

### Pattern Violations Found

1. **Hardcoded hosts** — elviajero and ozmontania hardcode `72.61.44.159` and `root` instead of using secrets
2. **Different SSH key names** — elviajero uses `DEPLOY_KEY`, others use `VPS_KEY`
3. **Wrong repo clones** — 3md-website and template-nextjs-client both clone superspuma (copy-paste bug)
4. **No CI anywhere** — 16 repos have a deploy workflow but only paragu-ai-builder, fun4me, Vete, and solstein-v2 have CI (lint/typecheck/test)
5. **No dependabot** on any client site
6. **No security scanning** on any client site deploy

### The 3 Deploy Strategies Used

| Strategy | Usage | Pros | Cons |
|----------|-------|------|------|
| **A: Build locally, SCP to VPS** (fun4me, maiyu, villamayor, dayah, 3md) | 5 repos | Clean, isolated | Slow, needs node_modules on runner |
| **B: Git pull + build on VPS** (elviajero, ozmontania) | 2 repos | Fast (no upload) | VPS does heavy build work, disk fills |
| **C: Builder pattern** (paragu-ai-builder) | 1 repo | CF Pages + VPS | Only for the builder, not clients |

### Proposed Reusable CI/CD Architecture

```
Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── ci-nextjs.yml             ← Reusable: lint + typecheck + test + build + secrets scan
│   ├── ci-python.yml             ← Reusable: ruff + pytest + secrets scan
│   ├── deploy-vps.yml            ← Reusable: build → scp Docker image → docker service update
│   └── deploy-vps-git-pull.yml   ← Reusable: git pull on VPS → build → restart
├── clients.json                  ← Registry: all 24 client sites with their deploy config
├── scripts/
│   ├── deploy.sh                 ← VPS-side script: takes site name, git url, docker image
│   └── sync-workflows.sh         ← GH API: push latest workflows to all client repos
├── templates/
│   ├── Dockerfile.nextjs
│   ├── Dockerfile.python
│   └── docker-compose.yml
└── README.md
```

Each client repo gets 2-3 lines:
```yaml
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml@main
  deploy:
    needs: ci
    uses: Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd/.github/workflows/deploy-vps.yml@main
    with:
      site: elviajero-comercio
    secrets: inherit
```

### What to Do With the Existing Workflows

| Repo | Action |
|------|--------|
| paragu-ai-builder | Keep as-is (gold standard). Point deploy runner at ci-cd scripts |
| Vete | Keep as-is (complex, multi-target). Only borrow patterns |
| fun4me, elviajero | Replace deploy.yml with ci-cd caller. Add CI first |
| maiyu, villamayor, dayah, 3md | Replace deploy.yml with ci-cd caller. Add CI first. Fix 3md bug |
| ozmontania | Replace deploy.yml with ci-cd caller (git-pull variant). Add CI |
| clinica-duerksen | Add deploy.yml via ci-cd caller |
| template-nextjs-client | Fix superspuma bug, replace with ci-cd caller |
| base | Add deploy-vps workflow via ci-cd |
| All other 14 repos | Add `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` calling ci-cd |

### What Stays As-Is

- **LosingLoonies-Youtube org** — 3 repos, unrelated (stock trading research)
- **Ivan's personal repos** — university work, early experiments, portfolio sites. No CI/CD needed
- **Archived repos** — already marked, no action
- **Public OSS** — telescope-ai, py-property-scraper, etc. — don't need deploy, but could use CI

### Recommended Priority

1. **Create ci-cd repo** with reusable workflows (1h)
2. **Set org secrets** — VPS_HOST, VPS_USER, VPS_KEY (30min)
3. **Fix 3md and template bugs** (15min)
4. **Roll out to 7 repos with existing deploy workflows** — replace with ci-cd callers (1h)
5. **Roll out to remaining 12 repos** — add deploy.yml (2h)
6. **Add CI to all** — ci-nextjs.yml reusable workflow (1h)
7. **Fix 22 cron jobs** (investigation needed)

function.arguments:
{"path": "/tmp/ci-cd/.github/workflows/ci-nextjs.yml", "content": "name: CI - Next.js

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      node-version:
        description: 'Node.js version'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '20'
      package-manager:
        description: 'npm or pnpm'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'npm'
      pnpm-version:
        description: 'pnpm version (only used if package-manager is pnpm)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '9'
      working-directory:
        description: 'Working directory for the Next.js app'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '.'
      supabase-url:
        description: 'Supabase URL for build (placeholder if not set)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '[REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]'
      supabase-anon-key:
        description: 'Supabase anon key for build (placeholder if not set)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'placeholder-anon-key'
      site-url:
        description: 'NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL for build'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'http://localhost:3000'

jobs:
  quality:
    name: Lint & Typecheck & Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: ${{ inputs.working-directory }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        if: inputs.package-manager == 'pnpm'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ inputs.pnpm-version }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ inputs.node-version }}
          cache: ${{ inputs.package-manager }}
          cache-dependency-path: |
            ${{ inputs.working-directory }}/package-lock.json
            ${{ inputs.working-directory }}/pnpm-lock.yaml

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          if [ "${{ inputs.package-manager }}" = "pnpm" ]; then
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          else
            npm ci
          fi

      - name: TypeScript check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Lint
        run: |
          if [ "${{ inputs.package-manager }}" = "pnpm" ]; then
            pnpm lint
          else
            npm run lint
          fi
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Build
        run: |
          if [ "${{ inputs.package-manager }}" = "pnpm" ]; then
            pnpm build
          else
            npm run build
          fi
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ inputs.supabase-url }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ inputs.supabase-anon-key }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: ${{ inputs.site-url }}

  test:
    name: Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: ${{ inputs.working-directory }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup pnpm
        if: inputs.package-manager == 'pnpm'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ inputs.pnpm-version }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ inputs.node-version }}
          cache: ${{ inputs.package-manager }}

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          if [ "${{ inputs.package-manager }}" = "pnpm" ]; then
            pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
          else
            npm ci
          fi

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          if [ "${{ inputs.package-manager }}" = "pnpm" ]; then
            pnpm test -- --run 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests configured"
          else
            npm test 2>/dev/null || echo "No tests configured"
          fi
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: ${{ inputs.supabase-url }}
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ inputs.supabase-anon-key }}
"}

on:

jobs:

        with:
          enable-cache: true

      - name: TruffleHog
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          path: ./
          base: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
          head: HEAD
"}

{"content": "#!/bin/bash
# deploy.sh — called from GH Actions via SSH
# Usage: deploy.sh <site-name> <github-repo>
set -euo pipefail

SITE=$1
REPO=$2
TMP_DIR="/tmp/$SITE"

echo "=== Deploying $SITE from $REPO ==="

# Clean and clone
rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"
git clone "https://github.com/$REPO.git" "$TMP_DIR"
cd "$TMP_DIR"

# Build Docker image
docker build -t "$SITE:prod" .

# Update Docker Swarm service (assumes service is named ${SITE}_web)
SERVICE="${SITE}_web"
if [REDACTED_DOCKER_CMD] --format '{{.Name}}' | grep -q "^${SERVICE}$"; then
  docker service update --force "$SERVICE"
  echo "=== Service $SERVICE updated ==="
else
  echo "=== Service $SERVICE not found — deploying fresh ==="
  docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml "$SITE" 2>/dev/null || \
    docker service create --name "$SERVICE" --publish 3000 "$SITE:prod"
fi

# Cleanup
rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"
echo "=== Deploy complete: $SITE ==="
", "path": "/tmp/ci-cd/scripts/deploy.sh"}

# setup-vps.sh — run once on the VPS to create deploy scripts
# Installs deploy scripts to /opt/stacks/ci-cd/scripts/
set -euo pipefail

DIR="/opt/stacks/ci-cd/scripts"
mkdir -p "$DIR"

cat > "$DIR/deploy.sh" << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
SITE=$1
REPO=$2
TMP_DIR="/tmp/$SITE"
rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"
git clone "https://github.com/$REPO.git" "$TMP_DIR"
cd "$TMP_DIR"
docker build -t "$SITE:prod" .
SERVICE="${SITE}_web"
if [REDACTED_DOCKER_CMD] --format '{{.Name}}' | grep -q "^${SERVICE}$"; then
  docker service update --force "$SERVICE"
else
  docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml "$SITE" 2>/dev/null || \
    docker service create --name "$SERVICE" --publish 3000 "$SITE:prod"
fi
rm -rf "$TMP_DIR"
SCRIPT
chmod +x "$DIR/deploy.sh"

cat > "$DIR/deploy-git-pull.sh" << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
SITE=$1
DIR=$2
cd "$DIR"
git pull
npm ci
npm run build
docker build -t "$SITE:prod" .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml "$SITE"
SCRIPT
chmod +x "$DIR/deploy-git-pull.sh"

echo "=== VPS deploy scripts installed to $DIR ==="

{"content": "FROM node:20-alpine AS base

# Install pnpm if needed
RUN npm install -g pnpm@9 2>/dev/null; exit 0

FROM base AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* pnpm-lock.yaml* ./
RUN if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; else npm ci; fi

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then pnpm build; else npm run build; fi

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
", "path": "/tmp/ci-cd/templates/Dockerfile.nextjs"}

Now the main README and the `.github` profile:

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"content": "# ci-cd — Ai-Whisperers Centralized CI/CD

**Single source of truth for all client site deployments.**  
Update a workflow here once → auto-synced to all client repos.

## Structure

```
.github/workflows/
  ci-nextjs.yml          — Reusable: lint + typecheck + test + build (Next.js)
  ci-python.yml          — Reusable: ruff + pytest + typecheck (Python)
  deploy-vps.yml         — Reusable: build on runner + SCP Docker to VPS
  deploy-vps-git-pull.yml — Reusable: git pull on VPS + build
  security-scan.yml      — Reusable: trufflehog + npm audit
  sync-workflows.yml     — Push latest workflows to all client repos on push

scripts/
  deploy.sh              — VPS-side: clone → docker build → service update
  deploy-git-pull.sh     — VPS-side: git pull → npm build → stack deploy
  setup-vps.sh           — Run once on VPS to install deploy scripts
  sync-workflows.js      — GH API: push workflows to all client repos

templates/
  Dockerfile.nextjs      — Standard Next.js multi-stage Dockerfile
  docker-compose.yml     — Standard Docker Compose with healthcheck

clients.json             — Registry of all client sites and their configs
```

## How It Works

1. **Each client repo** adds ONE file: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
2. That file calls the reusable workflows in this repo
3. When you update a workflow here, push to main → `sync-workflows` propagates it
4. No more copy-pasting deploy scripts across 24 repos

## Required Org Secrets

Set these in `Ai-Whisperers` org settings → Secrets and variables → Actions:

| Secret | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `VPS_HOST` | VPS IP (72.61.44.159) |
| `VPS_USER` | SSH user (root) |
| `VPS_KEY` | SSH private key |
| `SUPABASE_URL` | Shared Supabase URL |
| `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Shared Supabase anon key |

## Client Registration

Every client site must be added to `clients.json` with:
- `site` — Docker service/image name
- `repo` — GitHub repo path
- `url` — Live site URL
- `type` — `nextjs` or `python`
- `pm` — `npm` or `pnpm`
- `node` — Node.js version
- `docker_image` — Docker image tag
- `docker_service` — Docker Swarm service name
- `deploy_strategy` — `build-and-scp` or `git-pull`
", "path": "/tmp/ci-cd/README.md"}
